findings.justification: The filename from $_GET['filename'] is passed directly to set_filename() after only wp_unslash(), with no sanitization to strip path traversal sequences (../) or dangerous characters, potentially allowing directory traversal in the exported file path.

The filename from $_POST['filename'] is passed to set_filename() with only wp_unslash() and no sanitization, identical to the GET handler, enabling potential path traversal when the exporter writes or serves the generated file.

The download_export_file() handler fires on admin_init and relies solely on nonce verification without an explicit capability check, so any authenticated user who obtains or guesses a valid nonce can trigger a file download/export.

Array-typed POST parameters (columns, selected_columns, export_types, export_category) are passed via wp_unslash() only and forwarded to exporter methods without element-level type or value validation, trusting downstream sanitization that may not be guaranteed.

The woocommerce_export_get_ajax_query_args filter allows third-party plugins to inject arbitrary query arguments into the download URL returned to the browser, potentially enabling open-redirect or parameter-injection attacks if a malicious plugin is present.
